scenario: Team has reusable decision logic: "compute risk score." They wonder whether to model as BKM (Business Knowledge Model) or Decision (with Decision Service).
question: BKM vs Decision (in Camunda 8 -- Decision Service NOT supported)?

options.text: BKM and Decision are identical constructs in Camunda 8 DMN -- this approach has limitations. md:64); its result is not visible as a standalone entry in Operate. A Decision is a first-class Camunda 8 element invokable from BPMN via Business Rule Task zeebe:calledDecision or via the Orchestration Cluster REST/gRPC EvaluateDecision API. md:23-28.

md:64: "you can only reuse BKMs within the same decision"); body is a Literal Expression; result variable = BKM element name; not invokable from BPMN and not visible in Operate. Decision is a first-class element: invokable from BPMN via Business Rule Task zeebe:calledDecision or REST/gRPC EvaluateDecision; body is Decision Table OR Literal Expression. md:23-28. For shared logic within one DMN file use BKM; for BPMN-level invocation model a top-level Decision.

BKM (Business Knowledge Model) has been deprecated and removed from Camunda 8 DMN -- this claim does not fully apply. md:54-75. BKMs serve a specific and valuable purpose: they encapsulate reusable logic (Literal Expressions) within a DMN file, callable from other decisions in the same DRG. md:64), that constraint does not imply deprecation. 8 release with no planned removal.

Decision Service and BKM are the same construct in Camunda 8 -- this approach has limitations on two counts. First, they are conceptually distinct in the DMN specification: BKM is a function-like reusable formula, while Decision Service is a logical grouping that exposes a subset of decisions as a callable service. md:23-28 lists only four supported DMN element kinds: Decision, Input data, Knowledge source, BKM). Treating them as equivalent or assuming Decision Service is available in Camunda 8 will lead to modeling errors and deployment failures.

a.text: Incorrect. BKM vs Decision (Decision Service is NOT a Camunda 8 element -- Pattern #35): BKM (Business Knowledge Model): Purpose: Reusable logic within a single decision per dmn.md:64 verbatim "Currently, you can only reuse BKMs within the same decision" (Pattern #34 5th cumulative S6+S8+S11+S13+S15). Invoked via FEEL function-call syntax inside the same parent decision's literal expression (e.g. =calculateRiskScore(amount, history)). Parameters: optional typed formal parameters (amount: number, history: string) per dmn.md:62-74. Body: Literal Expression in C8 per dmn.md:23-25 (Boxed Function Expression is DMN-1.3 spec but NOT in C8 modeler -- Pattern #41). Result-variable name equals the BKM element name (dmn.md:68). Operate visibility: BKM evaluation result is NOT visible in Operate per dmn.md:56-58 verbatim "Viewing the result of BKM evaluation is currently not supported in Operate." Use cases: Common formula used by multiple decisions (e.g., tax calculation). Encapsulating decision logic for reuse. DRY within DMN model. Visual notation: Rectangle with "hat" (knowledge symbol). Connected to decisions via knowledgeRequirement (dashed line). Example BKM: Name: calculateTax. Parameters: amount, rate. Body: amount rate. Invocation from Decision: `feel total = grossAmount + calculateTax(grossAmount, taxRate) Decision: Purpose: First-class decision logic. Can be invoked from BPMN (Business Rule Task). Can be invoked standalone via API (per Set 14 Q28). Composable: one decision can depend on others (via information requirements). Use cases: High-level business decisions invoked from processes. Standalone "decisioning APIs" for external consumers. Multi-step decision flows. Decision Service (DMN-1.3 spec, NOT in Camunda 8 per dmn.md:23-28): DMN spec: logical grouping of decisions exposed as a service with declared inputs/outputs. Camunda 8 modeler does NOT support Decision Service (Pattern #35; only Decision / Input data / Knowledge source / BKM are listed in dmn.md:23-28). Engine does not invoke Decision Services: zeebe:calledDecision targets a single decisionId; EvaluateDecision REST POST /decision-definitions/evaluation and gRPC EvaluateDecision both take a single decision identifier. C8 substitute for "multiple decisions as one service": top-level Decision with informationRequirement to downstream decisions (DRG), invoked once by ID. Or orchestrate at BPMN level via multiple Business Rule Tasks. Comparison: | Aspect | BKM | Decision | |--------|-----|----------| | First-class | No (helper) | Yes | | Invocation source | Other decisions in DMN | BPMN + API + other decisions | | Parameters | Typed params | Inputs (via information requirements / mapped) | | Reuse scope | Within DMN model | Across DMN, BPMN, APIs | For the scenario (reusable "compute risk score"): If used only inside other decisions in same DMN model: BKM: clean encapsulation. If invoked from BPMN as standalone decision: Decision: first-class. If invoked both ways: Decision: more versatile. Other decisions can have information requirement on this decision. For the scenario specifically: "Compute risk score" sounds like a building block used by other decisions. BKM is a reasonable choice if used within DMN. Decision is appropriate if also called from BPMN. DMN modelling: BKM example: xml if history = "good" then amount 0.1 else amount * 0.3 Decision invoking BKM: xml if calculateRiskScore(loanAmount, creditHistory) ` Best practices: Use BKM for pure reusable logic within DMN. Use Decision for invokable units (from BPMN / API). DRD organisation: BKMs shown as helpers; Decisions as primary. Anti-patterns: Decision for trivial reusable logic: overhead. BKM for top-level business decisions: should be Decision. Inline logic everywhere instead of BKMs: violates DRY.
